content:
# Joshua Chapter 23 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 247-248 | **Source Image:** `../../source-texts/hebrew-OT/JOS_023_page_001.jpg`

---

**1** A long time had passed since YHWH gave Israel peace from all the enemies around them. Joshua had grown old, well along in years.

**2** So Joshua called together all of Israel — their elders, leaders, judges, and officials — and said to them, "I am old now, well along in years.

**3** You yourselves have seen everything that YHWH your God has done to all these nations for your sake, because YHWH your God is the one who has been fighting for you.

**4** Look — I have assigned to you by tribe all the remaining nations as your inheritance, from the Jordan all the way to the Great Sea in the west, including every nation I have already destroyed.

**5** YHWH your God will himself push them out ahead of you and drive them from your path, and you will take possession of their land, just as YHWH your God promised you.

**6** So be absolutely determined to observe and carry out everything written in the scroll of the Instruction of Moses, never straying from it one way or the other,

**7** and never mixing with these nations that still remain among you. Do not invoke the names of their gods, do not swear by them, do not serve them, and do not bow down to them.

**8** Instead, hold fast to YHWH your God, just as you have done up to this day.

**9** YHWH has driven out great and powerful nations ahead of you, and to this day no one has been able to stand against you.

**10** A single one of you can put a thousand to flight, because YHWH your God fights for you, just as he promised.

**11** So be very careful to love YHWH your God with your whole being.

**12** But if you ever turn away and ally yourselves with the remnant of these nations that remain among you — if you intermarry with them and mingle with one another —

**13** then know for certain that YHWH your God will no longer drive these nations out ahead of you. Instead, they will become a snare and a trap for you, a whip on your sides and thorns in your eyes, until you disappear from this good land that YHWH your God has given you.

**14** I am about to go the way everyone on earth must go. And you know deep down — with every fiber of your being — that not a single one of all the good things YHWH your God promised you has gone unfulfilled. Every one of them has come true for you; not one has failed.

**15** But just as every good thing YHWH your God promised has come about for you, so YHWH will bring on you every disaster he warned of, until he has wiped you out from this good land that YHWH your God has given you —

**16** if you break the covenant of YHWH your God that he charged you to keep, and go off to serve other gods and bow down to them. Then YHWH's anger will blaze against you, and you will quickly vanish from the good land he has given you."

---
## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

1. **v1 — "well along in years"**: The Hebrew idiom בָּא בַּיָּמִים literally means "come into the days." PNEUMA renders the meaning — advanced old age — in natural English rather than preserving the idiom's form.

2. **v4 — "the Great Sea in the west"**: Hebrew מְבוֹא הַשָּׁמֶשׁ means "the entrance/setting of the sun." Since this is simply a directional marker for English readers, PNEUMA renders it "in the west." The Great Sea is the Mediterranean.

3. **v6 — "scroll of the Instruction of Moses"**: Hebrew סֵפֶר תּוֹרַת מֹשֶׁה. תּוֹרָה means "instruction" or "teaching," not "law" in the narrow legal sense. "Scroll" reflects the actual medium rather than the anachronistic "book."

4. **v6 — "never straying from it one way or the other"**: The Hebrew "not turning right or left" is an idiom for total fidelity. PNEUMA conveys the meaning without the spatial metaphor.

5. **v7 — four prohibitions**: The Hebrew lists an escalating sequence — invoking names, swearing by, serving, bowing down — moving from casual speech to full-blown worship. The progression is preserved in natural English.

6. **v8 — "hold fast"**: Hebrew דבק ("cling/cleave") is the same word used for the marriage bond in Genesis 2:24. It communicates deep, committed attachment, not mere association.

7. **v11 — "with your whole being"**: Hebrew לְנַפְשֹׁתֵיכֶם. The word נֶפֶשׁ does not mean "soul" in the Greek philosophical sense. It refers to the entire living self — one's life, appetite, desire, and identity. "Whole being" captures this holistic sense.

8. **v13 — "a whip on your sides and thorns in your eyes"**: Hebrew שֹׁטֵט בְּצִדֵּיכֶם וּצְנִנִים בְּעֵינֵיכֶם. These vivid images of physical affliction are retained because they communicate powerfully even in English. The warning echoes Numbers 33:55.

9. **v14 — "go the way everyone on earth must go"**: Hebrew בְּדֶרֶךְ כָּל הָאָרֶץ is a dignified euphemism for death. PNEUMA makes the meaning accessible while preserving the solemn tone. David uses the same phrase in 1 Kings 2:2.

10. **v14 — "with every fiber of your being"**: Hebrew בְּכָל לְבַבְכֶם וּבְכָל נַפְשְׁכֶם, literally "with all your heart and with all your nephesh." Since both "heart" (לֵב) and "nephesh" (נֶפֶשׁ) refer to the inner self in Hebrew thought, PNEUMA consolidates them into one emphatic English expression rather than producing a wooden doublet.

11. **v15 — "every disaster he warned of"**: Hebrew כָּל הַדָּבָר הָרָע, literally "every evil word/thing." In context, these are the covenant curses threatened for disobedience. "Disaster he warned of" makes the covenantal logic explicit.

12. **v16 — "YHWH's anger will blaze"**: Hebrew וְחָרָה אַף יְהוָה. The word אַף literally means "nose" — anger pictured as nostrils flaring hot. "Blaze" captures the heat imagery without the anatomical detail.

13. **YHWH**: The divine name יהוה is always rendered YHWH, never replaced with "the LORD" or any other substitution. The name as written in the text is the name given to the reader.
